expectations.text: Script contains a model create command
expectations.evidence: Found: 'model create model_name = PENDULUM'

expectations.text: A rigid body part is created with part create rigid_body
expectations.evidence: Found: 'part create rigid_body name_and_position  part_name = .PENDULUM.LINK' and a second 'part create rigid_body mass_properties' for mass/inertia properties

expectations.text: At least one marker is created on the link and at least one on ground
expectations.evidence: Ground marker: 'marker create marker_name = .PENDULUM.ground.M_PIVOT'. Link markers: M_PIN, M_CM, M_TIP, and M_ROD_VIS all created with 'marker create marker_name = .PENDULUM.LINK.*'

expectations.text: A revolute joint is created using constraint create joint revolute with i_marker_name and j_marker_name
expectations.evidence: Found: 'constraint create joint revolute  joint_name = .PENDULUM.REV_PIVOT  i_marker_name = .PENDULUM.LINK.M_PIN  j_marker_name = .PENDULUM.ground.M_PIVOT'

expectations.text: A gravity field is created with gravity_field_name and a negative Y component
expectations.evidence: Found: 'forces create body_force gravity_field  gravity_field_name = .PENDULUM.GRAVITY  y_component_gravity = -9806.65'

expectations.text: Initial orientation is set to 45 degrees (or 45D)
expectations.evidence: Found: 'part modify rigid_body name_and_position  part_name = .PENDULUM.LINK  orientation = 0.0D, 0.0D, 45.0D'

expectations.text: All keywords are spelled out in full (no abbreviated forms like PART or CONST)
expectations.evidence: All commands use fully spelled keywords: 'model create', 'defaults units', 'forces create body_force gravity_field', 'marker create', 'part create rigid_body', 'constraint create joint revolute', 'part modify rigid_body', 'geometry create shape sphere/cylinder'. No abbreviated forms found.

expectations.text: Object names use dot-path hierarchy (e.g. .MODEL_NAME.PART_NAME.MARKER_NAME)
expectations.evidence: All object names use dot-path hierarchy: .PENDULUM.GRAVITY, .PENDULUM.LINK, .PENDULUM.ground.M_PIVOT, .PENDULUM.LINK.M_PIN, .PENDULUM.LINK.M_CM, .PENDULUM.LINK.M_TIP, .PENDULUM.REV_PIVOT, etc.

expectations.text: Uses force create element_like spring_damper (not single_component_force, bushing, or beam)
expectations.evidence: Script contains: `force create element_like spring_damper &` with spring_damper_name = .SPRING_MODEL.SPR_MAIN

expectations.text: The stiffness parameter is set to 5000
expectations.evidence: `stiffness = 5000.0` in the force create command; also confirmed in the parameters table: '5000.0 â€” Spring rate â€” 5000 N per mm of deformation'

expectations.text: The damping parameter is set to 100
expectations.evidence: `damping = 100.0` in the force create command; also confirmed in the parameters table: '100.0 â€” Damping coefficient â€” 100 NÂ·s/mm of velocity'

expectations.text: The length parameter (free length) is set to 250
expectations.evidence: `length = 250.0` in the force create command; table confirms: '250.0 â€” Free (natural) length â€” no force when the markers are 250 mm apart'

expectations.text: References both i_marker_name and j_marker_name
expectations.evidence: `i_marker_name = .SPRING_MODEL.PART_A.M_SPR_I` and `j_marker_name = .SPRING_MODEL.PART_B.M_SPR_J` both appear in the force create command

expectations.text: Explains or implies that spring_damper acts along the line joining the two markers
expectations.evidence: Two explicit statements: 'The force acts along the line joining the I and J markers.' (in How It Works) and 'The spring force is always along the line joining the two markers' (in Important Notes #3)

expectations.text: Uses the IMPACT() function (not BISTOP, STEP, or a manual IF expression)
expectations.evidence: The expression uses IMPACT(): `function = "IMPACT( DZ(...), VZ(...), 0.0, 1.0E5, 1.5, 50.0, 0.1)"`

expectations.text: The first argument measures displacement using DZ or equivalent (e.g. DZ(.MODEL.BODY.CM))
expectations.evidence: First argument is `DZ(.MY_MODEL.BODY.cm, .MY_MODEL.ground.FLOOR_REF, .MY_MODEL.ground.FLOOR_REF)`

expectations.text: The second argument measures velocity using VZ or equivalent
expectations.evidence: Second argument is `VZ(.MY_MODEL.BODY.cm, .MY_MODEL.ground.FLOOR_REF, .MY_MODEL.ground.FLOOR_REF, .MY_MODEL.ground.FLOOR_REF)`

expectations.text: The contact threshold value is 0.0
expectations.evidence: Third argument is `0.0` â€” described in the table as 'Floor is at Z = 0; contact activates when DZ < 0.0'

expectations.text: The stiffness value 1e5 (or 100000) is present
expectations.evidence: Fourth argument is `1.0E5` â€” described as 'Contact stiffness'

expectations.text: The exponent 1.5 is present
expectations.evidence: Fifth argument is `1.5` â€” described as 'Hertz exponent'

expectations.text: The damping value 50 is present
expectations.evidence: Sixth argument is `50.0` â€” described as 'Maximum damping coefficient'

expectations.text: The penetration depth 0.1 is present
expectations.evidence: Seventh argument is `0.1` â€” described as 'Damping ramp-up distance (mm)'

expectations.text: The expression is wrapped in double quotes as a FUNCTION= parameter value
expectations.evidence: The force element uses `function = "IMPACT( ...)"`  with the entire expression in double quotes

expectations.text: Script contains a model create command
expectations.evidence: Found: 'model create  &\n   model_name = simple_pendulum'

expectations.text: A rigid body part is created with part create rigid_body
expectations.evidence: Found: 'part create rigid_body name_and_position  &\n   part_name = .simple_pendulum.link'

expectations.text: At least one marker is created on the link and at least one on ground
expectations.evidence: Two markers on the link: '.simple_pendulum.link.pivot_I' and '.simple_pendulum.link.free_end'; one marker on ground: '.simple_pendulum.ground.pivot_J'

expectations.text: A revolute joint is created using constraint create joint revolute with i_marker_name and j_marker_name
expectations.evidence:
Found: 'constraint create joint revolute  &
   joint_name = .simple_pendulum.rev_joint  &
   i_marker_name = .simple_pendulum.link.pivot_I  &
   j_marker_name = .simple_pendulum.ground.pivot_J'

expectations.text: A gravity field is created with gravity_field_name and a negative Y component
expectations.evidence: Gravity is created with 'force create body gravitational' and y_component_gravity = -9806.65 (negative Y present), but the 'gravity_field_name' parameter is not specified in the command â€” 'model_name = .simple_pendulum' is used instead.

expectations.text: Initial orientation is set to 45 degrees (or 45D)
expectations.evidence: Found: 'orientation = -45.0, 0.0, 0.0' on the link part â€” the magnitude is 45Â°, representing the 45Â° initial angle from vertical (the negative sign indicates direction of rotation about Z).

expectations.text: All keywords are spelled out in full (no abbreviated forms like PART or CONST)
expectations.evidence: All commands use full keyword forms: 'model create', 'part create rigid_body', 'marker create', 'constraint create joint revolute', 'force create body gravitational', 'geometry create shape link', 'simulation single_run transient'. No abbreviated forms found.

expectations.text: Object names use dot-path hierarchy (e.g. .MODEL_NAME.PART_NAME.MARKER_NAME)
expectations.evidence: All object names use dot-path notation: '.simple_pendulum.link', '.simple_pendulum.link.pivot_I', '.simple_pendulum.link.free_end', '.simple_pendulum.ground.pivot_J', '.simple_pendulum.rev_joint', '.simple_pendulum.link.geom', '.simple_pendulum.last_run'

expectations.text: Uses force create element_like spring_damper (not single_component_force, bushing, or beam)
expectations.evidence: The response uses 'force create element spring_damper_bushing' â€” a hallucinated command that appends 'bushing' to the element type. The correct element type is 'spring_damper'. The assertion explicitly excludes 'bushing', and this command does not match the expected element type.

expectations.text: The stiffness parameter is set to 5000
expectations.evidence: stiffness = 5000.0 is present in the force create command.

expectations.text: The damping parameter is set to 100
expectations.evidence: damping = 100.0 is present in the force create command.

expectations.text: The length parameter (free length) is set to 250
expectations.evidence: free_length = 250.0 is present in the force create command.

expectations.text: References both i_marker_name and j_marker_name
expectations.evidence: i_marker_name = .MODEL.PART_1.SPRING_MAR_I and j_marker_name = .MODEL.PART_2.SPRING_MAR_J are both present in the command.

expectations.text: Explains or implies that spring_damper acts along the line joining the two markers
expectations.evidence: The overview states 'Adams computes the spring and damping forces along the line joining those two markers.' The notes also confirm 'Adams always acts along the I-to-J line.'

expectations.text: Uses the IMPACT() function (not BISTOP, STEP, or a manual IF expression)
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly uses IMPACT() throughout: 'FUNCTION = IMPACT(DZ(cm_marker_id, 0, 0), VZ(cm_marker_id, 0, 0), 0.0, 1.0E+05, 1.5, 50.0, 0.1, 0)'

expectations.text: The first argument measures displacement using DZ or equivalent (e.g. DZ(.MODEL.BODY.CM))
expectations.evidence: First argument is 'DZ(cm_marker_id, 0, 0)' â€” Z-displacement of the CM marker relative to the global origin

expectations.text: The second argument measures velocity using VZ or equivalent
expectations.evidence: Second argument is 'VZ(cm_marker_id, 0, 0)' â€” Z-velocity of the CM marker in the global frame

expectations.text: The contact threshold value is 0.0
The stiffness value 1e5 (or 100000) is present
expectations.evidence: Stiffness is listed in the parameters table as '1.0E+05' and used in the expression as the fourth argument

expectations.text: The exponent 1.5 is present
expectations.evidence: Exponent is listed as '1.5' in the parameters table and appears as the fifth argument in the IMPACT() call

expectations.text: The damping value 50 is present
expectations.evidence: Max damping is listed as '50.0' in the parameters table and appears as the sixth argument: '...1.5, 50.0, 0.1, 0)'

expectations.text: The penetration depth 0.1 is present
expectations.evidence: Penetration depth is listed as '0.1' in the parameters table and appears as the seventh argument: '...50.0, 0.1, 0)'

expectations.text: The expression is wrapped in double quotes as a FUNCTION= parameter value
expectations.evidence: The SFORCE definition shows 'FUNCTION = IMPACT(DZ(cm_marker_id, 0, 0), ...)' with no double quotes wrapping the expression
